text:
Admission Date:  1978-08-08              Discharge Date:   1986-10-16

Date of Birth:  2019-08-20             Sex:   M

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Jennifer
Chief Complaint:
head trauma

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none


History of Present Illness:
18yo RHM fell and hit L occiput after falling off
skateboard.  +LOC, no recollection of event, but OX3 for EMS.
Pt
developed nausea and vomiting since event.  Pt brought to Preston Memorial Hospital
ED where NCHCT revealed L basilar skull fracture.  Only
complaints in ED are L ear pain, nausea and vomiting.  Denies
headache or focal neurological complaints.


Past Medical History:
none.


Physical Exam:

VS:  afeb hr82  bp131/65  rr13  100%ra
General: alert, NAD; nauseated
HEENT:  hematoma behind L ear
CV:	RRR  s1s2     no m/r/g
Resp:	CTAB   no r/w/r
Abd:	+BS    Soft/NT/ND    no HSM/masses
Ext:	No c/c/e, distal pulses intact
Skin:	No lesions/bruises/rash
MS:	A&O x 3, interactive, appropriate, following all commands
Speech fluent w/o paraphasic errors
No evidence of neglect with visual or tactile stimulation
No apraxia evident
CN:	I ?????? not tested, II,III ?????? PERRL (3-2mm OU), VFF by
confrontation; III,IV,VI ?????? EOMI, no ptosis, no nystagmus;  V-
sensation intact to LT/PP, response to nasal tickle, masseters
strong symmetrically; VII ?????? no facial weakness/asymmetry; VIII ??????
hears finger rub B, but decreased (??????muffled??????) in L; IX,X ?????? voice
normal, palate elevates symmetrically; Lindsey ?????? SCM/Trapezii 12-17 B;

Motor:	nl bulk and tone, no tremor, rigidity or bradykinesia.
No
pronator drift.  No asterixis. Full power throughout UE and LE.
DTRs:	[**Name2 (NI) **] (C56)	BR (C6)	Tri (C7)	Pa (L34)	Ac (S12)  Plantar	
        L	2	2	2	2	2	  down
        R	2	2	2	2	2	  down
Sensory:	Intact to LT throughout
Coord:	finger tap rapid & symm, F&#1d25444d-e3e4-9792-1de4-53b987d6cb7e;N & FNF intact B.
Gait:	not assessed.


Pertinent Results:
1986-09-20 01:23AM BLOOD WBC-13.7*# RBC-4.50* Hgb-12.5* Hct-38.8*
MCV-86 MCH-27.8 MCHC-32.2 RDW-12.5 Plt Ct-226
1986-10-16 07:25AM BLOOD Glucose-91 UreaN-7 Creat-1.0 Na-139 K-3.9
Cl-103 HCO3-27 AnGap-13

Brief Hospital Course:
The patient was admitted for treatment by the SICU and the
neurological surgery service. CT exam showed minimally displaced
comminuted fracture through the left mastoid air cells and
extending into the temporal, parietal and occipital bones. This
was associated with a small amount of epidural air and
hemorrhage, with mild mass effect on the temporo-occipital
region with mild sulcal effacement. The patient was
neurologically intact but had hemotympanum and decreased hearing
in the left ear.  The exam was otherwise normal.  The patient
was hemodynamically stable. On hospital day 1, the foley was
discontinued, diet was advanced, and repeat CT showed stable
injuries, with no progression.  On hospital day 3,the patient
was discharged to home.

Medications on Admission:
None

Discharge Medications:
1. Phenytoin Sodium Extended 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule
PO TID (3 times a day) for 7 days.
Disp:*21 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*
2. Percocet 5-325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO every 4-6 hours:
hold for sedation, do not drive while on medication.
Disp:*15 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
3. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO every
6-8 hours: for constipation.
Disp:*15 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
traumatic head injury with skull fracture


Discharge Condition:
neurologically stable


Discharge Instructions:
DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS FOR HEAD INJURY
??????	Take your pain medicine as prescribed
??????	Exercise should be limited to walking; no lifting, straining,
excessive bending
??????	Increase your intake of fluids and fiber as pain medicine
(narcotics) can cause constipation
??????	Do not take any anti-inflammatory medicines such as Motrin,
aspirin, Advil, Ibuprofen etc. for one week.
??????	You have been prescribed an anti-seizure medicine, take it as
prescribed for one week

CALL IF YOU EXPERIENCE ANY OF THE FOLLOWING:

??????	New onset of tremors or seizures
??????	Any confusion or change in mental status
??????	Any numbness, tingling, weakness in your extremities
??????	Pain or headache that is continually increasing or not
relieved by pain medication
??????	Fever greater than or equal to 101?????? F

Followup Instructions:
PLEASE CALL 406-468-7661 TO SCHEDULE AN APPOINTMENT WITH DR.
Coffey TO BE SEEN IN 4 WEEKS.
YOU WILL NEED A CAT SCAN OF THE BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST
